channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Qld government backs proposal for zip-line over Brisbane River
item.description: A Queensland developer has proposed to build a wheelchair-accessible zip-line to cross the Brisbane River from Kangaroo Point to the City Botanic Gardens.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: An artist's impression of the proposed zip-line across the Brisbane River.

item.title: Jury retires in case of triple-fatal Greyhound bus crash driver
item.description: A jury retires to consider its verdict in the trial of a driver of a Greyhound bus which caused a triple-fatal crash on the Bruce Highway in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Chomicki

media:group.media:description: The jury in the trial of a Greyhound bus driver involved in a triple-fatal crash has retired.

item.title: More than 500 turtles discovered on the outskirts of far west NSW town
item.description: Hundreds of turtles are thriving near an outback NSW town, only months after the lake where they live was on the verge of drying up.
item.dc:creator: Lily McCure

media:group.media:description: Over 500 turtles have been found at Imperial Lakes Nature Park near Broken Hill.

item.title: Cruise of a lifetime becomes nightmare after hantavirus outbreak
item.description: It was meant to be the journey of a lifetime for Boston travel influencer Jake Rosmarin, crossing the south Atlantic Ocean on the MV Hondius, but the trip has turned into a nightmare after three people died in a hantavirus outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Fergus Gregg

media:group.media:description: Jake Rosmarin made a tearful statement regarding his experiences onboard the MV Hondius to an Instagram audience of 51,000 followers.

item.title: Man fighting removal to Nauru loses High Court challenge
item.description: A man fighting plans to remove him to Nauru loses his High Court challenge, after the government's deal to have the island take in people released from indefinite immigration detention is upheld.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Byrne

media:group.media:description: After his release from jail, the man was placed in indefinite immigration detention, only to be released after a critical High Court decision.

item.title: Family of injured hit-run victim pleads with driver to 'do the right thing'
item.description: Police are combing through video footage as they look for the mystery driver who allegedly struck an 18-year-old rugby league player along a rural highway.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Herrmann

media:group.media:description: Eathan Henry played junior rugby league for the Ravenshoe Tigers.

item.title: Meet the artist turning Venezuelan protest music into art
item.description: Unable to return to her homeland, Melbourne-based artist Nadia Hernández has reconnected with her culture through art.
item.dc:creator: Nicola Heath

media:group.media:description: Nadia Hernández in her exhibition 'Para verte mejor, en todo tiempo (To see you better, at all times)' at the Art Gallery of New South Wales, 21 March — 21 June 2026, artworks © Nadia Hernández, photo © Art Gallery of New South Wales, Jenni Carter.

item.title: Royal commission 'appalled' by man in swastika shirt out front of hearing
item.description: It comes as the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion enters its third day of hearings from Jewish Australians sharing lived experience.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Pengilley and Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: Police moved a man on from outside the royal commission because of the shirt he was wearing.

item.title: Ban on Craig Silvey's books hardened in public schools after guilty plea
item.description: WA Education Minister Sabine Winton confirms a temporary ban on Silvey's books being used as texts in public schools will now be made permanent.

media:group.media:description: Disgraced author Craig Silvey's books will be removed from WA schools permanently.

item.title: Two Pacific Islander farm workers dead after WA crash
item.description: The men died in a vehicle crash near Manjimup in the state's South West.
item.dc:creator: Georgia Loney

media:group.media:description: The crash happened on the outskirts of Manjimup, about 300km south of Perth.

item.title: Hospital bed shortage in Farrer hits crisis point as by-election looms
item.description: A major hospital is facing a significant bed shortage in the electorate of Farrer, where residents will soon vote in a by-election.
item.dc:creator: Erin Somerville

media:group.media:description: Many voters want to see a new hospital built.

item.title: Arrests planned when ISIS-linked families return to Australia
item.description: Flights have been booked for families linked to Islamic State fighters returning from Syria to Australia, with women in the group facing arrest and investigation after they arrive.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Asher

media:group.media:description: The women and children left the Al Roj camp in Syria late in April, with plans to travel to Australia.

item.title: Divers retrieve body after car submerged at Sydney's Royal National Park
item.description: Police divers have located the body of a male passenger after a car submerged underwater at the Royal National Park in Sydney's south.
item.dc:creator: Digby Werthmuller

media:group.media:description: The body was recovered following a multi-agency search for a male passenger whose car submerged in water.

item.title: Publican stands trial accused of starting pub fire after increasing premiums
item.description: A former publican has been accused of increasing the maximum value of the pub's contents insurance policy before deliberately setting it on fire in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Isla Evans

media:group.media:description: Bombala's Globe Hotel was destroyed by fire in June 2022.

item.title: Iran maintains Strait of Hormuz blockade with 'annoying' mosquito fleet
item.description: Iran's so-called mosquito fleet of small, speedy attack boats has become an increasingly aggressive nuisance for the US Navy, as it attempts to break the blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Annika Burgess

media:group.media:description: A US Navy helicopter hovers over Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps speed boats.

item.title: Is an 'inclusive' gender category the solution to IOC ban on trans women?
item.description: For Phoebe and Luce, joining an LGBTQI+ social climbing group was transformative. But as a trans woman and non-binary person, they have faced barriers to participating in elite competition and are calling for change.
item.dc:creator: Kate O'Halloran

media:group.media:description: Phoebe feels accepted as a trans woman in social climbing circles.

item.title: Healthcare leader named as Tasmania's new governor
item.description: Caroline Wells will become the 30th Governor of Tasmania after receiving formal approval from King Charles III, replacing Barbara Baker AC.

media:group.media:description: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff made the announcement on Wednesday morning.

item.title: The ideal age to start swimming lessons for your child
item.description: Swimming ability is declining in Australia, and experts say knowing the right age to enrol your children in lessons — as well as what to do if you can't access them — is key to improving water safety.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Scott

media:group.media:description: Many learn-to-swim schools offer lessons for children as young as six months, but do they need to start then?

item.title: Scientists discover species of ancient 'dimpled' koala in WA
item.description: A new analysis of fossilised koala skulls and bones has revealed an extinct species of koala lived in WA until a few thousand years ago.
item.dc:creator: Peter de Kruijff

media:group.media:description: Modern koalas have lived in Yanchep National Park, in the north of Perth, since a colony was established in 1938.

item.title: Italian PM says deepfake depicting her in lingerie is a political attack
item.description: Giorgia Meloni shares an AI-generated photo of her posing on a bed and says her political opponents have been using such images to try to undermine her.

media:group.media:description: Giorgia Meloni shared the deepfake online to warn people not to believe everything they saw.

item.title: Rahm pays fine to return to DP World Tour as LIV doubt grows
item.description: Jon Rahm will be eligible for Europe in next year's Ryder Cup after agreeing to pay fines and play events on the DP World Tour this season, a move that also triggered speculation about his future with LIV Golf beyond 2026.

media:group.media:description: Jon Rahm has agreed to pay his fines to the DP World Tour to receive re-entry.

item.title: Latest Melbourne business set alight owned by onetime MAFS contestant
item.description: Two men are believed to have jumped a fence, smashed glass windows and set fire to a vehicle inside a Port Melbourne car dealership before fleeing, according to police.

media:group.media:description: Police and firefighters at the scene in Port Melbourne.

item.title: Exclusive look inside artist's historic home at centre of court battle
item.description: The resident trying to take adverse possession of artist Frederick McCubbin's historic former home says he wants to bring in the "right people" to fix it up.
item.dc:creator: Anna McGuinness

media:group.media:description: Trevor Hoare has filed for adverse possession of Fontainebleau on Mount Macedon.

item.title: Arsenal close in on historic double by reaching Champions League final
item.description: Arsenal reach the Champions League final for the second time in their history with a 1-0 home win over Atletico Madrid sealing a 2-1 aggregate victory in the semi-final.

media:group.media:description: Arsenal scraped a 1-0 win over Atletico Madrid to reach the Champions League final.

item.title: Witness describes frantic rescue attempt during deadly boating tragedy
item.description: Darragh Hunt was fishing at Ballina when the incident that claimed three lives unfolded before his eyes.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ross, Lachie McLeod, and Bruce MacKenzie

media:group.media:description: Two volunteers died when this NSW Marine Rescue boat capsized at Ballina.
